scope.field_scan: all .text references to controller displacements +0xB9 and +0xBC

controller_plus_0xB9.writer: 0x140156776 writes DIL after the totp map lookup in 0x140156676..0x14015678C

controller_plus_0xBC.writer: 0x140156750 stores the dword map-lookup output after the totp map lookup in 0x140156676..0x14015678C

input_registers.rdx: packet or byte buffer cursor object

primary_inputs: length or inline-string metadata at controller +0xA8/+0xB7
cached totp result flag at controller +0xB9
cached totp result dword at controller +0xBC
selector_byte_rules: If len(controller +0xA0) == 6 and controller +0xB8 != 0, write selector 1.
If len(controller +0xA0) == 6 and controller +0xB8 == 0, write selector 3.
If len(controller +0xA0) != 6 and controller +0xB9 != 0, write selector 0.
If len(controller +0xA0) != 6 and controller +0xB9 == 0, write selector 2.
payload_rules: Selector 0 loads controller +0xBC, applies the global byte-order transform guarded by 0x140B2708C, and writes four bytes to the packet buffer.
Selectors 1 and 3 parse the six-character controller +0xA0 string through 0x1407C01C0 with base 10 and write the compact three-byte value.
Selector 2 advances the packet cursor by four bytes without writing a fresh dword in this helper.

direct_calls.role: base-10 parse or encode for the six-character +0xA0 code path

callers.register_flow: RCX remains the controller and RDX remains the packet object through the wrapper before calling 0x140154CA0.
callers.context: This wrapper is reached from previously mapped state-0x50 packet-builder branches around 0x14015808F and 0x14015879C.

callers.register_flow: RCX = RDI and RDX = stack packet object at [RBP - 0x48] before calling 0x140154CA0.
callers.context: This secondary packet path references template or descriptor 0x140E59848, checks [RDI +0x20] against selector 2, calls 0x1401548A0, appends the same totp selector payload through 0x140154CA0, then serializes via 0x14016B1F0.

resolved_chain: controller +0xC0 is converted to a decimal string and used as the key into the totp map rooted at controller +0xAA8
0x140156676 stores successful map-lookup output into controller +0xBC and marks controller +0xB9
0x140154CA0 is the later packet-side consumer that decides whether the auth payload uses cached +0xBC, a six-character +0xA0 code, or a reserved/skip selector
relationship_to_previous_totp_map_milest.conclusion: The client does not treat the 947 TOTP/auth payload as one plain text field. It has at least four selector modes, and the atotp/totp map path feeds the selector-0 cached-dword mode.

opennxt_947_impact: OpenNXT 947 login/auth handling should model the TOTP/auth extension as a selector byte followed by selector-specific payload, not as a single string-only field.
Selector 0 likely carries the cached dword recovered through the controller +0xC0 to totp-map lookup chain, while selectors 1 and 3 carry a compact value derived from a six-character decimal string.

Selector 2 appears to reserve or skip four payload bytes in this helper; server-side compatibility work should verify whether those bytes are expected to be zero, previously staged, or ignored by the receiver.

The secondary path at 0x1401574EF should be correlated against OpenNXT packet templates because it builds a local packet object with descriptor 0x140E59848 before appending the same selector helper output.
next_targets: Resolve writers and semantics of controller +0xB8 to name why the six-character +0xA0 code path chooses selector 1 versus selector 3.
Resolve writers and source semantics of the string-like object at controller +0xA0.
Name the secondary packet descriptor or template at 0x140E59848 and the serializer call 0x14016B1F0.
Map the upstream stream dword that is inserted into the totp map and later recovered into controller +0xBC.
Correlate the recovered selector/payload modes with OpenNXT 947 login/auth packet decoders and fixtures.
